rationale: The single substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26 questioning whether the reduce_scatter hasattr checks are sufficient verification) is essentially echoed by Candidate Y's finding that init_vllm_model_parallel_group is never exercised for real — only mocked — leaving reduce_scatter's actual contract untested; Candidate X instead affirmatively concludes the reduce_scatter/device_communicator wiring is correct and never surfaces it as a gap. Y's findings are all grounded with specific file/line citations and concrete fixes (add docstring, add real-call test, document collective assumption), giving it higher actionability, though some severity labels ('major' for missing docstrings) look inflated. X is well-reasoned and precise but functions mostly as an approval with one minor non-blocking nit, so it captures little of what the human reviewer actually flagged. Neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
